title: The Phillips curve: foundational concepts
description: Practice what you know about the Phillips curve model in this exercise.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following curves illustrates the same relationship between the price level and unemployment as the short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) curve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The short-run Phillips curve (SRPC)
The long-run Phillips curve (LRPC)
The aggregate demand (AD) curve
The long-run aggregate supply (LRAS) curve
The production possibilities curve (PPC)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the relationship that the short-run Phillips curve (SRPC) illustrates?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the tradeoff between unemployment and inflation
the tradeoff between liquidity and earning interest
The maximum sustainable amount of production in an economy
the natural rate of unemployment is independent of changes in the inflation rate
The full employment output is independent of changes in the price level

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which curve implicitly has the same relationship as the short-run Phillips curve (SRPC)**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Short-run aggregate supply (SRAS)
Long run aggregate supply (LRAS)
Aggregate demand (AD)
The money supply curve
The supply of loanable funds

assessment_items.question_content:
**An economy is always operating at a point on which of the following curves?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Short-run Phillips curve (SRPC)
Long-run Phillips curve (LRPC)
Production Possibilities curve (PPC)
Long-run aggregate supply curve (LRAS)
At the intersection of the short-run Phillips curve (SRPC) and the long-run Phillips curve (LRPC)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following curves illustrates that there is a tradeoff between inflation and the unemployment rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The short-run Phillips curve (SRPC)
The long-run Phillips curve (LRPC)
The aggregate demand (AD) curve
The production possibilities curve (PPC)
The demand for money

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the long-run Phillips curve (“LRPC”)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: vertical at the natural rate of unemployment
downward sloping showing the tradeoff between inflation and unemployment
Upward sloping showing the positive relationship between inflation and unemployment
Upward sloping showing the positive relationship between output and the price level
Vertical at the current rate of unemployment

assessment_items.question_content:
The current state of the economy of Te Rehunga is represented in the graph shown here:

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2c4e0847a080fb46b9fb5bd436887b8f5ab497b8)

Let point B in the options below represent the current combination of inflation and the unemployment rate. 

**Which of the following graphs best depicts the Phillips curve model of Te Rehunga based on this AD-AS model?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A vertical axis labeled "inflation percentage" ranges from zero to a maximum value, displaying a downward-sloping line representing the short-run Phillips curve (SRPC), intersecting a horizontal axis labeled "unemployment percentage." A dashed line extends horizontally from the point where the SRPC intersects the vertical axis, marking a specific point labeled "B." Above this dashed line is a vertical line denoting the long-run Phillips curve (LRPC), indicating a specific relationship between inflation and unemployment in the long run.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/171eaf592a0a6bd0d907384b80b4a74e56911bea)

![A downward-sloping line labeled "LRPC" starts from the upper left and extends to the lower right. A vertical dashed line intersects the "LRPC" line at point "B," extending downward to a point on the x-axis. Another downward-sloping line labeled "SRPC" parallels the "LRPC" line, intersecting the x-axis at a point labeled "UR percentage." The vertical axis is labeled "inf. percentage," and the horizontal axis is labeled with "UR percentage." The overall diagram presents a relationship between the variables represented by the axes and the two lines.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0404b3d07f2cf15addd570f0929c6f9f845d3ec7)

![The visual displays a graph with a vertical axis labeled "inf. %" representing inflation percentage and a horizontal axis labeled "UR%" indicating unemployment rate percentage. A downward-sloping line labeled "SRPC" intersects the vertical axis, indicating a relationship between inflation and unemployment. A point marked "B" is on the downward slope of the "SRPC" line, accompanied by a dashed vertical line extending to the horizontal axis, intersecting with a blue solid line labeled "LRPC." The graph is framed within a rectangular box.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5a5150c5884d67442251b5f47940c4cb15bfd834)

![A Cartesian coordinate system representing the relationship between inflation percentage on the vertical axis and unemployment rate percentage on the horizontal axis. The long-run Phillips curve is depicted as a downward-sloping straight line labeled "LRPC," indicating a negative relationship. The short-run Phillips curve is represented as a downward-sloping straight line labeled "SRPC" that intersects the y-axis at a higher value than "LRPC." A vertical dashed line labeled "B" extends from a point on the "SRPC" to the horizontal axis, indicating a specific unemployment rate percentage. The labels "inflation percentage," "unemployment rate percentage," "LRPC," and "SRPC" are positioned near their corresponding lines. All elements are clearly defined without any extraneous information.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/39b421df7e1854ad5e1f6ba1bd20846791b25fa6)

![A Cartesian coordinate system where the vertical axis labeled "inf. %" represents inflation percentage and the horizontal axis labeled "UR%" represents unemployment rate percentage. A downward-sloping straight line labeled "LRPC" intersects a dashed vertical line labeled "B" at a point on the unemployment rate axis. Below the unemployment rate axis, a downward-sloping dashed line labeled "SRPC" intersects the vertical line at the same point "B," indicating the relationship between inflation and unemployment rates through these lines.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c4ae5301f2a6a0da82dacac0fc56b52e855deae5)

assessment_items.question_content:
**In a model of the long-run Phillips curve (LRPC), what is the shape of the Phillips curve and where should it be placed?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Vertical at the natural rate of unemployment
Vertical at the current rate of unemployment
Vertical at full employment output
Horizontal at the current rate of inflation
Horizontal at the natural rate of inflation

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume that an economy was initially in long-run equilibrium but then experiences a change in economic conditions. Point K in the graph shown here represents the current state of an economy.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/88573acb37b79d4fdb16eabda07c5312583bfc92)

**Which of the following best describes the current state of this economy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An economy experiencing a recessionary gap due to a negative aggregate demand shock
An economy producing on its production possibilities curve
A economy that is producing beyond its production possibilities curve
An economy in long-run equilibrium
An economy experiencing an inflationary gap due to a negative short-run aggregate supply shock

assessment_items.question_content:
The economy of Rotorua is currently in long-run equilibrium. The current rate of unemployment is equal to 7%, and the current rate of inflation is 3%.

**Which of the following models best illustrates this information using the Phillips Curve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![The image presents a graph with a downward-sloping line representing the Short-Run Phillips Curve, labeled "SRPC," intersecting a horizontal dotted line at the level of inflation percentage labeled "inf. %" and indicating a point at three percent. A vertical line, labeled "LRPC," intersects this horizontal line at a value of seven percent on the horizontal axis labeled "UR%" for unemployment rate percentage. The intersection of the SRPC and the horizontal line indicates a specific relationship between inflation and unemployment. The graph includes axes with percentages marked, clearly showing the inflation and unemployment rate scales.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4c2de902db942980cbd5098bc3399be065cd846f)

![A downward sloping line representing the Short-Run Phillips Curve intersects a vertical dashed line labeled "Long-Run Phillips Curve." The intersection is at a point marked "Short-Run Phillips Curve" along the horizontal axis, which is labeled "Unemployment Rate Percentage." The vertical axis is labeled "Inflation Percentage" with a marker at three percent and another intersection point near the seven percent marker.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0f34e02fec7d664108154fac0e2bc75ba688f66e)

![A downward-sloping line represents a relationship on a graph with the vertical axis labeled "inflation percentage" ranging from 0% to 3% and the horizontal axis labeled "unemployment rate percentage" ranging from 0% to 7%. A dashed horizontal line intersects the graph at the point where it meets the unemployment rate axis at 7%, while a dashed vertical line intersects the inflation axis at 3%. The line is marked with the label "Short-Run Phillips Curve" (SRPC) on the right side and "Long-Run Phillips Curve" (LRPC) on the left side.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8aa6f7d2c639a1dab98729e86ad081aaf719f3fa)

![A downward-sloping straight line representing a negative relationship between a variable labeled "UR%" on the horizontal axis and "inf. %" on the vertical axis. The axes are labeled with values; the vertical axis indicates a value of three percent, and the horizontal axis indicates a value of seven percent. A dashed horizontal line intersects the downward-sloping line at this point, labeled "SRPC." The graph does not include any grid lines or markers beyond the axes and labels.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/95c1202f0688c5e6a7be6970cb32d5c40d403545)

![A downward-sloping line represents a relationship between two variables, labeled as inflation percentage on the vertical axis and unemployment percentage on the horizontal axis. The vertical axis has a value of three percent marked, while the horizontal axis has a value of seven percent marked. A horizontal dashed line intersects the downward slope at the point labeled as Short Run Phillips Curve, or SRPC, which is identified at three percent inflation and seven percent unemployment. The line continues downward, eventually reaching a value labeled as Long Run Phillips Curve, or LRPC, on the vertical axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f6b044ea5bdab64c5122f0dd8a58a71d1ceb2cbd)

assessment_items.question_content:
The natural rate of unemployment in Hamsterville is 4%. The current rate of unemployment is 2%, and the inflation rate is 6%. 

**Which of the following models best illustrates the inflationary gap that Hamsterville is experiencing using point K to represent the current state of the economy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A two-dimensional graph with vertical and horizontal axes labeled as "inflation percentage" and "unemployment rate percentage," respectively. The vertical axis ranges from 0% to 6%, while the horizontal axis extends from 0% to 4%. A downward-sloping linear curve labeled "Short Run Phillips Curve" crosses the vertical axis at the 6% mark and intersects the horizontal axis at the 2% mark. A vertical dashed line labeled "K" is drawn at the 4% mark on the horizontal axis, extending to the 6% mark on the vertical axis. Additionally, a line labeled "Long Run Phillips Curve" is positioned vertically at the 4% unemployment rate percentage.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5cdd73252a1e0c8f78df32c299372aa385bc792b)

![The diagram shows a Cartesian coordinate system with inflation percentage on the vertical axis labeled as "inf. %" and unemployment rate percentage on the horizontal axis labeled as "UR%." A downward-sloping line represents the relationship between these two variables, labeled "SRPC," which stands for Short-Run Phillips Curve. A vertical line intersects this downward slope at a point labeled "K," which denotes a specific inflation percentage. A horizontal dashed line extends from point K towards the vertical axis, and another curve is labeled "LRPC," which stands for Long-Run Phillips Curve, running vertically at the point of intersection.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cadb5d20837fb2fb8b7edc4b01fab4714c92afe8)

![A graph with an x-axis labeled "UR percent" and a y-axis labeled "inf percent" features a downward-sloping line representing "SRPC," intersecting a horizontal dashed line at the point labeled "K." A vertical line labeled "LRPC" intersects the y-axis. The line "SRPC" begins from the top left and descends toward the bottom right, indicating a negative relationship as it approaches the lower right quadrant. The vertical line indicating "LRPC" is positioned directly above point "K." The graph outlines two interest rates with a clear demarcation of where the two lines intersect.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cd46c16a0ee04dbb1db30d46d3b647b28aa9f146)

![The image shows a two-dimensional graph with a vertical axis labeled "inflation percentage" and a horizontal axis labeled "Unemployment Rate percentage." A downward-sloping linear curve representing the Short-Run Phillips Curve intersects the horizontal axis at a point labeled "K," which is marked with a dashed line. There is also a vertical line marked "Long-Run Phillips Curve," extending upward from the intersection point of the horizontal axis. The vertical line is labeled "LRPC," and the downward-sloping curve is labeled "SRPC."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e15343a75567ba6a5e68cba27f925a7d25424d25)

![A graph with labeled axes where the vertical axis represents inflation percentage and the horizontal axis represents unemployment rate percentage. The curve labeled "SRPC" slopes downward from left to right, indicating an inverse relationship between inflation and unemployment. A straight line labeled "LRPC" is vertical, indicating that in the long run inflation does not affect unemployment. A vertical dashed line intersects the SRPC curve at a point labeled "K," indicating a specific point of intersection between the two curves.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0faf7d47d122cea98169dcac496538fcf614fb54)

assessment_items.question_content:
The following economic measurements were collected during May 2018 in Hamsterville:

Measure | Value in 2018
-|-
Current real GDP | \$210 million
Potential real GDP | \$200 million
Current rate of unemployment | 5%
Natural rate of unemployment | 6%
Current rate of inflation | 4%
Current real interest rate | 7%

**Which of these graphs correctly illustrates the economy’s *current* condition using a Phillips curve model?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A two-dimensional graph with the vertical axis labeled "inflation rate percentage" ranging from zero to at least four percent, and the horizontal axis labeled "unemployment rate percentage" ranging from zero to at least five percent. A downward-sloping straight line intersects the graph, labeled "SRPC." Point A is marked on the graph at the coordinates where the unemployment rate percentage is five percent and the inflation rate percentage is four percent, with dashed lines extending horizontally and vertically from point A to the respective axes.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b2b19f8a586cc10c82997549429edce783095f0a)

![The graph displays a downward-sloping line representing a relationship between inflation percentage on the vertical axis and unemployment percentage on the horizontal axis. The vertical axis is labeled with a tick mark at 4 percent, while the horizontal axis has a tick mark at 6 percent. A dotted horizontal line extends from the 4 percent mark on the vertical axis to intersect the line at a point labeled A. The line also intersects a point labeled SRPC, indicating the Short Run Phillips Curve at the point where both axes are marked. The graph is framed within a square outline.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bb16bb2a8d6e19ea171269494b0d77be4d05a4a4)

![A graph with an x-axis labeled "UR%" ranging from zero to five percent and a y-axis labeled "inf. %" ranging from zero to an unspecified maximum percentage. A vertical line intersects the x-axis at five percent and the y-axis at four percent, indicating point A where the two lines meet. A dashed horizontal line extends from point A to the left, intersecting the y-axis at four percent. The label "LRPC" is placed vertically along the y-axis, and the graph has a border that appears red and dashed.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6e25800cca52d75456c32147add7ae37861860e0)

![A two-dimensional graph with a vertical axis labeled "inflation percentage" and a horizontal axis labeled "unemployment percentage." The vertical axis has a marked point at four percent, while the horizontal axis has a marked point at six percent. A solid vertical line extends upward from the point marked "A," which is located at the intersection of the dashed horizontal line and a solid vertical line. The segment from point "A" to the horizontal axis is dashed, representing a fixed value, and the curve labeled "LRPC" runs vertically through point "A." The entire graph is framed by a rectangular box.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2543a4a3c4ad427f3f6f79965cd15cb68565dfd3)

![A two-dimensional graph with vertical and horizontal axes labeled as "inf. %" and "UR %," respectively. The vertical axis is marked at 4 percent, while the horizontal axis is marked at 5 percent. A point labeled "A" is located at the intersection of the two axes, with dashed lines extending from point A to both axes. The curve labeled "SRPC" is shown at a horizontal position slightly above point A. The overall layout includes a bordered area indicating a specific graph area but excludes decorative elements.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/befc41767536b359123e3205d90f914f7bb3bd53)

assessment_items.question_content:
The Hamsterville government recently engaged in contractionary fiscal policy. 

**Which of the following Phillips curve models correctly illustrates the short-run impact of contractionary fiscal policy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A graph features a downward-sloping curve labeled as SRPC on the right vertical axis, representing a relationship with the horizontal axis labeled as UR percent. Two points are marked on the curve: point one positioned higher on the y-axis and to the left, and point two lower on the y-axis and further to the right, connected by a dashed vertical line indicating a relationship between the points. Horizontal dashed lines extend left from each point to the vertical axis. The axes are marked with arrows for directionality, and the graph includes numerical annotations near the points.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/abf51beaa8701d7ffaaf33566abdb21db75e7c0f)

![The visual shows a graph with a horizontal axis labeled "x" and a vertical axis labeled "f of x." A dashed horizontal line, representing a constant value, intersects the vertical axis. A continuous curve is depicted as a downward-opening parabola, with its vertex at the highest point in the graph, decreasing symmetrically on both sides of the vertex. The curve approaches but does not intersect the horizontal line, indicating a limiting behavior as x increases or decreases. The data suggests that the function approaches the horizontal line without crossing it, demonstrating a specific relationship between x and f of x across the plotted range.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e45a6323b70a8165f36cdfa24801c31b7480eedc)

![A graph featuring two axes with the vertical axis labeled "inflation percentage" and the horizontal axis labeled "unemployment percentage." A downward-sloping line represents the Long-Run Phillips Curve, indicated as "LRPC," which intersects two horizontal dashed lines at two distinct points. The first point is annotated as "1" and is located on the left dashed line at a higher value of inflation percentage. The second point is labeled as "2" and is positioned on the right dashed line, showing a lower value for inflation percentage. A vertical dashed line extends downward from point "2" to intersect the horizontal axis, marked as "SRPC1."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4b34e972935908eae08f60c0d6caae9e98f908ee)

![A graph with a vertical axis labeled "inflation percentage" and a horizontal axis labeled "unemployment rate percentage" features two downward-sloping short-run aggregate supply curves, denoted as "SRPC1" and "SRPC2," positioned adjacent to each other. A long-run aggregate supply curve, labeled "LRPC," intersects the vertical axis at one point. The graph includes two horizontal dashed lines indicating specific levels of inflation, marked as "1" and "2," with a vertical blue dashed line drawn downward from the intersection of "LRPC" and "1" to meet the horizontal axis. An arrow points leftward from the vertical line to highlight key juncture points in the graph.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/82783effa547eacd530f6ee4f85f2f5aad68627d)

![A graph displaying a downward-sloping line labeled "Long Run Phillips Curve" intersecting two downward-sloping lines labeled "Short Run Phillips Curve One" and "Short Run Phillips Curve Two" at points marked "1" and "2" with dashed horizontal lines extending to the y-axis, which represents inflation percentage, and a horizontal x-axis representing unemployment percentage. The graph shows the relationship between inflation and unemployment with a vertical dashed line from point "1" towards the x-axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ea41a732933ffe1d6f79360a24b2affffb38fe03)

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume the current rate of unemployment is 5% and the natural rate of unemployment is 7%. The expected rate of inflation is 4%.

**Which of the following correctly illustrates the current state of the economy using long-run and short-run Phillips curves?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A graph with two axes, the vertical axis labeled "inflation percentage" ranging from 0% to a maximum marked at the top, and the horizontal axis labeled "unemployment percentage" ranging from 0% to a maximum marked on the right. The graph contains two labeled lines: one labeled "Long Run Phillips Curve" (LRPC) represented as a vertical line at approximately 7% on the horizontal axis, indicating a fixed level of inflation at that unemployment rate, and another labeled "Short Run Phillips Curve" (SRPC) that slopes downward from the left to the right, intersecting both axes. A point labeled "X" is marked at the intersection of a dashed horizontal line at 4% inflation and a dashed vertical line at 5% unemployment. The point "X" indicates the current state of inflation and unemployment.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/044d03969988d3725d575f826ec0d3c0235e4bc9)

![A two-dimensional graph depicting a downward-sloping line labeled "Short-Run Phillips Curve" with a vertical intercept at approximately four percent inflation and a horizontal intercept at seven percent unemployment. A dashed vertical line intersects the x-axis at five percent unemployment, and a dashed horizontal line extends from this point to meet the "Short-Run Phillips Curve" at point labeled "X." A dashed horizontal line is positioned at four percent inflation, intersecting the y-axis. The area above this line is labeled "Long-Run Phillips Curve."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/69093add7e229219ccaf8a24ccc7fec665a9fb6f)

![A two-dimensional graph with a horizontal axis labeled "UR percent" ranging from zero to seven and a vertical axis labeled "inf percent" ranging from zero to four. A downward-sloping line labeled "SRPC" extends diagonally from the upper left to the lower right. A vertical line labeled "LRPC" is drawn at the point where the "UR percent" is equal to five. A point marked "X" is located at the intersection of the "SRPC" line and the "LRPC" line, with dashed lines extending horizontally and vertically to indicate the coordinates of the point, which intersect at five on the horizontal axis and four on the vertical axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8365dc306cd9a88146e680889e53ae06a726ff9b)

![A graph with labeled axes where the vertical axis represents the variable labeled “UR percentage” and the horizontal axis represents the variable labeled “Inflation percentage.” A downward-sloping line representing "Short-Run Phillips Curve" intersects a vertical line representing "Long-Run Phillips Curve" at point labeled “X.” The horizontal line extends to 4 percent on the vertical axis and crosses the vertical line at 5 on the horizontal axis, while the horizontal line intersects the downward-sloping line at 7 on the horizontal axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cd0456e82365fcf548740d70bc57d39d0c1720f2)

![A coordinate graph is displayed with the vertical axis labeled "inf. %" ranging from 0% to 4%, including a dashed horizontal line at the 4% mark. The horizontal axis is labeled "UR%" ranging from 5% to 7%, with a dashed vertical line at the 5% mark. A blue line labeled "SRPC" slopes downward from left to right, intersecting the horizontal axis at approximately 7%. A vertical line labeled "LRPC" aligns with the 5% point on the horizontal axis. A point marked "X" is located at the intersection of the dashed lines, with a dashed connection indicating its coordinates at 4% inflation and 5% unemployment. The overall structure outlines the relationships between inflation percentage and unemployment percentage.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a1c781dc7cd5c493c5acd8b6b99c8bce25c5e2dc)

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume the current rate of unemployment is 9% and the natural rate of unemployment is 5%. The expected rate of inflation is 4%.

**Which of the following correctly illustrates the current state of the economy using long-run and short-run Phillips curves?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![The graph consists of two primary curves: a downward-sloping line labeled “Short-Run Phillips Curve” represented as “SRPC” and a vertical line labeled “Long-Run Phillips Curve” represented as “LRPC.” The vertical axis denotes inflation percentage, with tick marks indicating values of 4% and an additional label “inf l.” The horizontal axis denotes unemployment percentage, with tick marks indicating values of 5% and 9%. A point labeled “Z” is located at the intersection of the SRPC and a horizontal dashed line at 4% inflation. The dashed horizontal and vertical lines create clear reference points for the intersection within the coordinate system.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/089103f121c3602f991e8001284efc13d6114fc8)

![A two-dimensional graph with the vertical axis labeled "inflation percentage" and the horizontal axis labeled "unemployment percentage." A downward-sloping line represents the Short-Run Phillips Curve, labeled "SRPC," which intersects the vertical axis at the "inflation percentage" of nine and crosses the horizontal axis at the "unemployment percentage" of five. A second horizontal line indicates an inflation percentage of four; it intersects the short-run Phillips curve. A dashed vertical line extending from the intersection point meets the horizontal axis at five. A point labeled "X" is marked at the intersection of the dashed lines. A segment representing the Long-Run Phillips Curve is labeled "LRPC" and is vertical, showing the relation of inflation and unemployment in the long run.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/75d5a492ea10f06ac652753a37ae6801516c9c99)

![A graph with two axes labeled as inflation percentage on the vertical axis and unemployment percentage on the horizontal axis. The vertical axis ranges from zero to at least four percent, marked at four percent. The unemployment percentage axis ranges from zero to at least nine percent, with a point marked at five percent. A downward-sloping line labeled "Short-Run Phillips Curve" extends from the upper left towards the lower right. A vertical line labeled "Long-Run Phillips Curve" is located at the unemployment percentage of nine. The intersection point of the dashed lines at the coordinates five percent unemployment and four percent inflation is marked as point X.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e0bf4e288581423a596e6c603d67d4dc62021b24)

![A two-dimensional graph with a vertical axis labeled "UR percent" ranging from 0 to a maximum value, and a horizontal axis labeled "Inflation percent" ranging from 0 to 9. A downward-sloping line labeled "LRPC" intersects a vertical dashed line at "X" positioned above the horizontal dashed line at "4 percent." This point "X" corresponds to a horizontal position at "5" on the Inflation percent axis and a vertical position just below "4 percent" on the UR percent axis. A dashed line extends horizontally from "X" to the left, and a vertical dashed line extends downward from "X" to meet the horizontal line at "4 percent," below which the point labeled "SRPC" is situated. The graph contains a border that does not contribute to the data visualized.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/975d3da43f8ccb3d271ae5535462deef447b162e)

![A graph with a vertical axis labeled "inf. %" ranging from 0 to at least 9 percent, and a horizontal axis labeled "UR%" ranging from 0 to at least 9 percent. A vertical line labeled "LRPC" intersects the "UR%" axis at 5 percent, and a downward-sloping line labeled "SRPC" crosses the vertical line at approximately 4 percent on the "inf. %" axis. A point labeled "Z" is marked at the intersection of the dashed horizontal line at 4 percent and the dashed vertical line extending from the "UR%" axis at 9 percent. The graph features gridlines for better data visualization.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cd6ab6d9a560d80cd34a362d7be69ecac08112c8)

assessment_items.question_content:
The current state of the economy of Johnsrudia is represented by point Z in the graph shown here.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following statements are true based on this graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A two-dimensional graph with an x-axis labeled "UR%" and a y-axis labeled "inf. %" features a downward-sloping line representing the short run Phillips curve, intersecting a vertical line labeled "LRPC," which stands for long run Phillips curve. A point marked "Z" lies on the short run Phillips curve at the coordinates where the x-axis reads 5% and the y-axis reads 4%. Dashed horizontal and vertical lines extend from point Z, with the horizontal line intersecting the inf. % axis at 4% and the vertical line intersecting the UR% axis at 5%. The right side of the graph extends to 8% on the x-axis, indicating the upper range of the unemployment rate.

choices.content: The current rate of inflation is lower than the expected rate of inflation.
The economy has an inflationary gap.
The SRPC will shift to the right in the long run.
The LRPC will shift to the right in the long run.
Borrowers are unexpectedly better off.

assessment_items.question_content:
The current state of the economy of Johnsrudia is represented by point Z in the graph shown here.



[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following statements is correct based on this graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A two-dimensional graph with an x-axis labeled "UR%" and a y-axis labeled "INF%". A downward-sloping line labeled "SRPC" intersects the y-axis at eight percent and the x-axis at three percent. A vertical line labeled "LRPC" intersects the x-axis at eight percent and the y-axis at a point below the SRPC line. A point labeled "Z" is located at the coordinates three percent on the x-axis and seven percent on the y-axis. A dashed line extends horizontally from point Z to the y-axis and vertically down to the x-axis, intersecting it at three percent.

choices.content: Inflation is higher than expected.
There is a recession.
The SRPC will shift to the left in the long run.
Unemployment is higher than the natural rate.
Lenders are unexpectedly better off.
